_note: Updated by the audio director after each task. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Define the sonic identity of Ashen Hollow — instrumentation direction, emotional register, reference palette
priorities.note: Without a defined sonic identity, music and SFX will be produced without a coherent direction. This is the audio equivalent of the art bible — it needs to exist before any audio production starts.
priorities.proposed_solution: Deliver a sonic identity memo (instrumentation, emotional register, references) as the audio bible before commissioning music or SFX.

priorities.title: Specify the UI audio feedback layer for the Workbench tools
priorities.note: The Workbench tools currently have no audio feedback. Before any public-facing release, the key interactions (entity placement, mode switch, export complete) should have short, non-intrusive audio cues.
priorities.proposed_solution: Write a concise Workbench UI SFX spec for placement, mode changes, and export complete, sized for web audio implementation.

priorities.title: Research and select the adaptive audio middleware approach for Ashen Hollow
priorities.note: Metroidvania music needs to adapt to game state — exploration, combat, boss fights, quiet moments. The middleware choice affects how music is authored and integrated. This decision should be made before any music production begins.
priorities.proposed_solution: Compare adaptive music approaches and middleware options, then recommend one that fits solo-founder engineering capacity.

priorities.title: Draft the audio brief for the opening biome
priorities.note: Blocked on the game director and Creative confirming the first biome. Audio direction should follow the visual and narrative direction, not lead it.
priorities.proposed_solution: When the first biome and creative direction lock, draft the opening biome music and ambience brief for level and narrative.

founder_decisions.title: Which adaptive audio approach is right for Ashen Hollow?
founder_decisions.note: Horizontal re-sequencing (stems), vertical layering, or a middleware system (FMOD, Wwise)? Each has different production implications and cost. Needs a decision before music is commissioned.

actions.name: Audio Brief
actions.description: Sound design brief for a new game element or feature — sonic identity, emotional register, reference points, trigger events, implementation notes.
actions.trigger: A new game element, UI pattern, or feature needs sound design

actions.name: SFX Spec
actions.description: Full SFX spec — trigger conditions, duration, frequency range, spatial properties, variation requirements.
actions.trigger: A specific sound effect is approved for production

actions.name: Music Direction Memo
actions.description: Music direction memo for a biome or scene — instrumentation, adaptive system design, emotional arc, reference tracks.
actions.trigger: A new biome, major narrative moment, or boss fight is entering production
